channel.title: Exodus on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Exodus on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Exodus 1
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 1

1 Israel’s descendants and their households that went to Egypt with Jacob: 2 Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, 3 Issachar, Zebulun, Benjamin, 4 Dan, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher: 5 70 people (though Joseph was already there)

6 That whole generation died, 7 but Israel’s descendants multiplied and became so numerous they filled the country.

item.title: Exodus 2
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 2

The Birth of Moses

1 A descendant from Levi married a woman from Levi, 2 and she had a son. When she saw he was such a handsome baby, she hid him for three months. 3 When she couldn’t hide him anymore, she got a papyrus reed basket and covered it with tar. She put him in it and set it in the reeds by the bank of the Nile. 4 His sister stood a distance away to see what would happen to him. 5 Pharaoh’s daughter came down to bathe at the Nile with her attendants. As she walked along the river, she saw the basket in the reeds and had her maid bring it to her.

item.title: Exodus 3
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 3

Moses and the Burning Bush

            1 Moses was pasturing Jethro’s/Reuel’s flock and led it to the west side of the open country around Horeb/Sinai, the mountain of God. 2 The angel of the LORD appeared to him in a blazing bush. Moses looked up and saw the bush on fire, but it didn’t burn up. 3 He said, “I have to go over and see this curious thing. Why isn’t it burning up?” 4 When the LORD saw him coming to look, he called from the bush,

item.title: Exodus 4
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 4

Signs for Moses

1 Moses answered, “What if they don’t believe me, ‘The LORD hasn’t really appeared to you!’?”

            2 “What’s in your hand?”

            “A staff.”

            3 “Throw it on the ground.”

            He threw it on the ground, and it became a snake. Moses jumped back.

item.title: Exodus 5
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 5

Moses and Aaron Speak to Pharaoh

1 After that, Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh, “The LORD, Israel’s God, says to let his people go so they can celebrate a festival to him in the open country.”

2 Pharaoh said, “Who is the LORD that I should obey him? I don’t know him. I’m not going to let them go.”

item.title: Exodus 6
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 6

Promise of Deliverance

1 The LORD told Moses,

“Now you’ll see what I’ll do to Pharaoh. He’ll have to let them go. I’ll make him send them out of his country. 2 I’m Yahveh. 3 I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as ‘God Almighty’ [El-Shaddai]. I didn’t make myself known to them by my name ‘Yahveh.’ 4 I made a covenant with them to give them Canaan, where they were living as nomads. 5 I’ve heard the Israelites’ groaning because the Egyptians are holding them in slavery, and I’ve remembered my covenant. 6 Tell the Israelites, ‘I’ll take you out from under Egyptian bondage and redeem you with power and great acts of judgment. 7 I’ll take you to be my people and be your God. You’ll know I’m the LORD your God who brought you out from under the burdens the Egyptians put on you. 8 I’ll take you to the land I swore to give Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’”

item.title: Exodus 7
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 7

Aaron’s Staff Becomes a Snake

            1 The LORD told him,

“I’m making you like God to him, and your brother Aaron will be your prophet. 2 Say what I tell you, and Aaron will tell Pharaoh to let Israel leave his country. 3 But I’ll harden his heart so I can multiply my signs and wonders. 4 When he won’t listen to you, I’ll lay my hand on Egypt and take out my people with tremendous acts of judgment. 5 The Egyptians will know I’m the LORD.”

item.title: Exodus 8
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 8

The Plague of Frogs

            1 The LORD said to Moses,

“Go tell Pharaoh the LORD says to let his people go so they can serve him. 2 If you don’t, he’ll strike the whole country with frogs. 3 The Nile will teem with them. They’ll come up into your house, your bedroom, your bed, your officials’ houses and on your people, in your ovens and kneading bowls. [ 4 ]’”

item.title: Exodus 9
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 9

The Plague on Livestock

            1 The LORD told Moses,

“Go tell Pharaoh, ‘The LORD God of the Hebrews says to let his people go so they can serve him. 2 If you keep holding them, 3 he’ll bring a severe plague on your livestock in the pasture—horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks. 4 But he’ll distinguish Israel’s livestock from Egypt’s. Nothing will die that belongs to the Israelites.’”

item.title: Exodus 10
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 10

The Plague of Locusts

1 Then the LORD told Moses,

“Go to Pharaoh; I’ve hardened his and his officers’ hearts so I can perform these signs among them 2 and you can tell your sons and grandsons how I mocked the Egyptians with my signs, and so you can know I’m the LORD.”

item.title: Exodus 11
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 11

Plague on the Firstborn

1 The LORD told Moses,    

“I’m going to bring one more plague on Pharaoh and Egypt. After that, he’ll let you go. When he does, he’ll drive you out completely. 2 Tell each man and woman to ask their neighbors for silver and gold articles.”

item.title: Exodus 12
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 12

The Passover Instituted

1 The LORD told Moses and Aaron,

2 “This month will be the first month of your year. 3 Tell the assembly of Israel that on the tenth of this month, each family is to take a lamb according to their families. 4 If a family is too small for a lamb, that family and a nearby neighbor can take a lamb the size the number of people can eat. 5 It needs to be an unblemished male a year old. Take it from the sheep or goats. 6 Keep it till the fourteenth of the month.

item.title: Exodus 13
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 13

Dedication of the Firstborn

1 The LORD told Moses, 2 “Set aside every firstborn human and animal.”

3 Moses told the people,

“Think back to today to when you came out of slavery in Egypt. With a strong hand the LORD took you out of here. Don’t eat anything with yeast in it. 4 Today in the month Abib, you’re about to leave. 5 When the LORD takes you to the territory of the Canaanite, Hittite, Amorite, Hivite, and Jebusite—the land he swore to your ancestors he’d give you, a land flowing with milk and honey—observe this rite in this month. 6 Eat bread without yeast for a week. The seventh day, observe a feast to the LORD. 7 Don’t have anything with yeast in it among you or in your territory. 8 Tell your son that day, ‘It’s because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.’ 9 It will serve as a sign to you on your hand, a reminder on your forehead, that the Law of the LORD may be in your mouth, because he brought you out with a tremendous demonstration of power. 10 Keep this ordinance every year at its scheduled time.

item.title: Exodus 14
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 14

1 The LORD said to Moses,

2 “Tell the Israelites to turn around and camp at Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea across from Baal-zephon. 3 Pharaoh will say you’re wandering aimlessly; the open country has boxed you in. 4 That’s how I’ll harden his heart, and he’ll chase you. He and his army will honor me, and the Egyptians will know I’m the LORD.”

item.title: Exodus 15
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 15

Moses’ Song of Triumph

            1 Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the LORD:

                  “I’ll sing to the LORD because he’s triumphed gloriously;

                        he’s thrown the horse and its rider into the sea.

                  2 The LORD is my strength and song;

item.title: Exodus 16
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 16

Manna and Quail

1 The fifteenth day, second month after they left Egypt, they went from Elim to the Wilderness of Sin, between Elim and Sinai, 2 They criticized Moses and Aaron in the desert, 3 “If only the LORD had killed us in Egypt when we sat by pots of meat and ate bread till we were full. You’ve brought us out to this empty place to starve to death.”

item.title: Exodus 17
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 17

Water from the Rock

            1 Then the LORD told the community to travel from the Wilderness of Sin and camp at Rephidim. They didn’t have any water and 2 complained to Moses, “Give us some water.”

            “Why are you complaining to me and testing the LORD?”

            3 But they were thirsty, “Why bring us up from Egypt to let us, our little ones, and our livestock die from thirst?”

item.title: Exodus 18
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 18

Jethro Visits Moses

            1 Jethro/Reuel, priest in Midian, Moses’ father-in-law, heard what all God had done for Moses and his people in bringing them out of Egypt. 2 He’d taken Moses’ wife Zipporah after Moses sent her away 3 with her two sons Gershom (because he said, “I’ve been a ‘foreigner’ in a foreign land”) 4 and Eliezer (because he said, “My father’s ‘God helped’ me and delivered me from Pharaoh’s sword”). 5-6 Jethro/Reuel came to Moses with his wife and two sons, where Moses camped at the mountain of God. He sent word that he was bringing them. 7 Moses went out to meet him, bowed and kissed him. They asked each other how they were doing and went into the tent. 8 Moses told him what all the LORD had done to Pharaoh and the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, the hardships they’d had on the journey, and how the LORD had delivered them. 9 Jethro/Reuel was overjoyed at the LORD’s goodness to Israel. 10 He said,

item.title: Exodus 19
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 19

At Mount Sinai

1 The same day of the third month after Israel left Egypt, they came to the Wilderness of Sinai. 2 They’d set out from Rephidim and came to camp at the mountain. 3 Moses went up to God, and the LORD called to him from the mountain,

“Tell Jacob’s house, 4 ‘You’vepl seen what I did to the Egyptians, how I carried you on eagles’ wings and brought you to me. 5 If you’ll obey me and keep my covenant, you’ll be my special possession among the nations. The whole world belongs to me, 6 but you’ll be a kingdom of priests, a holy nation.’”

item.title: Exodus 20
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 20

The Ten Commandments

            1 Then God spoke,

                  2I am the LORD your God who brought you out of slavery in Egypt.

                  3Do not havef any god but me.

4 Do not makef any carved image, any likeness of what is in the sky, on the ground, or in the water 5 to worship or serve. I am an exclusive God. I visit the consequences of parents’ sins on their descendants for three or four generations that hate me. 6 I am kind to thousands who love me and obey me.

item.title: Exodus 21
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 21

Laws About Slaves

1 “Present these ordinances to the people.

2 “If you buy a Hebrew slave, he’ll serve six years and go free on the seventh without pay. 3 If he came alone, he’ll go out alone. If he has a wife, they’ll go out together. 4 If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children belong to her master, and the slave will go out alone. 5 But if the slave says he loves his master, his wife and children, and won’t leave them to be a free man, 6 his master is to take him to the judges—to the door or doorpost—and pierce his ear with an awl. Then he’ll serve him permanently.

item.title: Exodus 22
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 22

Protection of Property

            1 “If someone steals a cow or sheep, slaughters it or sells it, he has to pay five cows for one cow and four sheep for one sheep. 3bIf he doesn’t have anything, sell the thief for the theft. 4 If what was stolen is found alive in the thief’s possession—cow, donkey, or sheep—the thief will pay double.

item.title: Exodus 23
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 23

                  1 “Don’t pass on false rumors, join sinners in giving malicious testimony, 2 or follow the crowd in doing something bad. In a dispute, don’t join other people to pervert justice. 3 Don’t be partial toward a poor person in a dispute.

4 “If you run onto your enemy’s ox or donkey wandering around, return it. 5 If you see a donkey that belongs to someone that hates you lying helpless under its load, free it.

item.title: Exodus 24
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 24

The Covenant Confirmed

1 Then the LORD told Moses, “Come up to me and worship at a distance—you, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and the 70 elders. 2 Yousg alone will approach me; the others and the community can’t approach me.”

3 Moses recounted to the people the LORD’s ordinances, and they answered together, “We’ll do what he’s said.”

item.title: Exodus 25
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 25

Contributions for the Tabernacle

1 The LORD said to him,

2 “Tell the Israelites to take up an offering for me as their hearts move them: 3 gold, silver, and bronze; 4 blue, purple, and scarlet material; fine linen, goat hair, 5 rams’ skins dyed red, dolphin skins, acacia wood, 6 olive oil for lighting, spices for anointing oil and fragrant incense, 7 onyx stones and setting stones for the ephod and pouch.  8 Have them build a sanctuary so I can live among them. 9 Build the Tabernacle and its furniture like I show you.                                            Ex 25:1-9

item.title: Exodus 26
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 26

The Tabernacle

1 “Make the Tabernacle with 10 curtains of finely twined linen and blue, purple, and scarlet material. Make them with cherubs, a skilled weaver’s work. 2 All the curtains will be 42 x 6ft. 3 Make 2 sets of 5 joined curtains. 4 Make blue loops on the edge of the outer curtain in the first set; do the same with the second set. 5 Make 50 loops in the one curtain, and 50 loops on the edge of the second set. Put the loops across from each other. 6 Make 50 gold clasps and join the curtains together with them so the Tabernacle makes a unit.                                                                                                 Ex 26:1-6

item.title: Exodus 27
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 27

The Bronze Altar

            1 “Make an altar out of acacia wood, 7½ x 7½ x 4½ft high 2 with a horn on each corner. Make them one piece with the altar and overlaid with bronze.

3 “Make bronze pails for removing the ashes, bronze shovels, basins, forks, and fire pans.

                  4“Make a network of bronze grating for it, and on the network put four bronze rings at the corners. 5 Put the grating under the ledge of the altar so it reaches halfway up the altar.

item.title: Exodus 28
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 28

The Priestly Garments

1 “From the Israelites, have your brother Aaron and his sons Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar come to you to serve as priests. 2 Make holy vestments for Aaron, glorious and beautiful. 3 Talk to skilled artisans that I’ve given wisdom for making them and consecrate them for performing service to me. 4 Make these vestments for Aaron and his sons: a pouch, ephod, robe, tunic made out of checkered work, a turban, and a sash. 5 The artisans are to use gold; blue, purple, and scarlet material; and fine linen.

item.title: Exodus 29
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 29

Dedication of the Priests

1 “This is how you’re to consecrate them as priests to me: take a young bull, two rams without blemish, 2 bread without yeast, and cakes mixed with olive oil, wafers without yeast anointed with oil and made out of fine wheat flour. 3 Put them in a basket and bring them with the bull and two rams. 4 Take Aaron and his sons to the door and give them a bath. 5 Put on Aaron the tunic, ephod robe, ephod, chest pouch, and skillfully woven band of the ephod. 6 Put the turban on his head, and the holy crown on the turban. 7 Then pour the anointing oil on his head. 8 Put tunics on his sons. 9 Put sashes around him and his sons and tie the caps on them. They’ll exercise the priesthood by permanent statute.

item.title: Exodus 30
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 30

The Altar of Incense

1 “Make an altar out of acacia wood as a place to burn incense: 2 18 x 18 x 36in with horns in one piece with it. 3 Overlay it with pure gold on its sides, top, and horns; make a molding around it. 4 On two sides put two gold rings under its molding on opposite ends for carrying it. 5 Make the poles out of acacia wood overlaid with gold. 6 Put the altar in front of the curtain that’s near the Ark of Testimony—in front of the mercy seat that’s on top of the Ark—where I’ll meet you. 7-8 Aaron will burn fragrant incense on it every morning and evening and trim the lamps. Burn incense to me continuously throughout your generations. 9 Don’t offer foreign incense, burnt offering, or meal offering on this altar. Don’t pour any liquid offering on it. 10 Throughout your generations, Aaron will make atonement on its horns once a year with the blood of the sin offering. It’s most holy to me.

item.title: Exodus 31
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 31

Bezalel and Oholiab

31[:1] 2 “I’ve specifically called Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, from the tribe of Judah. 3 I’ve filled him with my spirit in wisdom, understanding, and knowledge in all kinds of craftsmanship

                       4to make artistic designs for work in gold, silver, and bronze,

item.title: Exodus 32
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 32

The Golden Calf

1 When Moses hadn’t come down from the mountain for a long time, the people gathered around Aaron and said, “Make us a god to go ahead of us. We don’t know what’s happened to this Moses that brought us out of Egypt.”

2 Aaron said, “Take the gold earrings off of your wives, sons, and daughters, and bring them to me.” [ 3 ] 4 He took them, and with a graving tool shaped a molten calf.

item.title: Exodus 33
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 33

            1 The LORD told Moses,

“Leave here and go to the land I swore I’d give the descendants of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. 2 I’ll send an angel ahead of you to drive out the Canaanite, Amorite, Hittite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite. 3 Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey. I won’t go up with you, because you’re stubborn, so I won’t destroy you on the way.”

item.title: Exodus 34
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 34

The New Stone Tablets

1 The LORD told Moses,

“Carve out two stone tablets like the first ones, and I’ll write on them what was on the tablets you shattered. 2 Be ready by morning. Come up to the top of Mount Sinai and present yourself 3 alone. Nobody can be on the mountain. Flocks and herds can’t graze by it.”

item.title: Exodus 35
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 35

Sabbath Regulations

1 Moses gathered the community and said,

“The LORD has commanded these things: 2 ‘You can work for six days, but set the seventh day aside to him as a complete rest. Whoever works on it will be executed. 3 Don’t start a fire in your houses on the Sabbath.’”

item.title: Exodus 36
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 36

2 Moses called Bezalel, Oholiab, and their artisans to begin the work. 3 They received from him the contributions for building the sanctuary. [ 4 ] 5 They told him, “The people are bringing more than we need.” 6 He circulated a message throughout the camp that they didn’t need to contribute anything more. [ 7 ]

item.title: Exodus 37
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 37

The Ark

            1 Bezalel made the Ark out of acacia wood: 45 x 27 x 27ins high. 2 He overlaid it with pure gold inside and out, made a gold border around it, 3 and cast 4 gold rings for it, 2 on each side. 4 He shaped poles out of acacia wood overlaid with gold 5 and put them in the rings for carrying it.

item.title: Exodus 38
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 38

The Altar of Burnt Offering

1 Then he built the altar for burnt offering out of acacia wood: 7½ x 7½ x 4½ft high. 2 He formed horns on its four corners one piece with it and overlaid it with bronze. 3 He made bronze utensils for the altar: pails, shovels, basins, meat hooks, and fire pans. 4 He fashioned a bronze grating under the edge of the altar that reached halfway up, 5 and cast 4 rings for the 4 ends of the grating to hold the poles. 6 He formed the poles out of acacia wood overlaid with bronze, 7 and inserted them into the rings for carrying it. He made it hollow with planks.

item.title: Exodus 39
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 39

The Priestly Garments

            1 From the blue, purple, and scarlet material, they made finely woven vestments for Aaron to perform service in the Holy Place—like the LORD commanded Moses.

2 He made the ephod out of gold; blue, purple, and scarlet material; and finely twined linen. 3 They hammered out gold sheets and cut them into threads that were woven in with the blue, purple, and scarlet material; and the fine linen—an artisan’s work. 4 They made attaching shoulder pieces for the ephod—attached at the two upper ends. 5 The skillfully woven band on it had the same workmanship: gold; blue, purple, and scarlet material; and finely twined linen.  6 They made the onyx stones, set in delicate gold ornamentation. They engraved the onyx stones like the engravings on a signet, showing the names of Israel’s sons. 7 He placed them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for them.

item.title: Exodus 40
item.description:
Exodus Chapter 40

Setting Up the Tabernacle

            1 Then the LORD told Moses,

2 “The first day, first month, set up the Tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting. 3 Put the Ark of Testimony in it and screen it off with the curtain.

4 Take the table in and arrange what goes on it;
